home.title: Value Investing Analytics
home.description1: This report is generated using a modernized Value Investing methodology inspired by {buffett} and {graham}. Each company is evaluated based on its financial strength, profitability, operational efficiency, and whether the stock appears undervalued relative to its intrinsic value.
home.description2: Each company receives a {score} across fundamentals, efficiency, capital structure, and intrinsic valuation.
home.criteriaIntro: The scoring model is based on a weighted evaluation of the following key criteria:

criteria.epsTrend: Consistent growth in Earnings Per Share (EPS), indicating durable earning power.
criteria.epsCagr: EPS compound annual growth rate (CAGR) above 10%, showing strong long-term growth.
criteria.roic: Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) above 10%, reflecting efficient capital allocation.
criteria.grossMargin: Gross profit margin above 60%, suggesting a durable competitive advantage.
criteria.fcfPositive: Positive Free Cash Flow (FCF), confirming the ability to generate excess cash.
criteria.dividendPayout: Dividend payout ratio below 60%, balancing shareholder return and reinvestment.
criteria.debtToEquity: Debt-to-Equity ratio below 2, showing a conservative capital structure.
criteria.operatingMargin: Operating margin above 10%, representing operational efficiency.
criteria.currentRatio: Current ratio above 1.5, demonstrating short-term financial health.
criteria.interestCoverage: Interest coverage above 3, ensuring ability to service debt.
criteria.debtEbitda: Debt/EBITDA ratio below 3, indicating sustainable leverage.
criteria.grahamNumber: Market price below the Graham Number, a classical fair-value benchmark.
criteria.dcf: DCF intrinsic value above the current market price, suggesting undervaluation.
